sources.author: Swyx
sources.headline: AI Researcher & Developer Advocate
sources.post_summary: Discusses the evolution of AI agents from hype to practical deployment, highlighting key frameworks like LangChain and AutoGPT for building autonomous systems. Shares insights on challenges in reliability and scaling for production use. Emphasizes the need for better developer tools to streamline agent orchestration.

sources.author: Andrej Karpathy
sources.headline: Founder, Eureka Labs; Former Director of AI at Tesla
sources.post_summary: Explores the infrastructure required for training and deploying large-scale AI models, focusing on GPU clusters and efficient data pipelines. Predicts a surge in demand for specialized AI infra tools amid the agent boom. Calls for open-source contributions to democratize access.

sources.author: Nat Friedman
sources.headline: Co-founder of GitHub
sources.post_summary: Highlights emerging developer tools powered by AI for code automation and agentic workflows, such as Cursor and Devin. Argues that these tools will transform software engineering by handling repetitive tasks. Shares examples of productivity gains from early adopters.

sources.author: Amanda Scalisi
sources.headline: VP of AI Strategy at Microsoft
sources.post_summary: Outlines how Azure's AI infrastructure is enabling enterprise-grade AI agents for business automation. Details integrations with tools like Copilot for seamless developer experiences. Predicts widespread adoption in the next quarter.

sources.author: Eliot Peper
sources.headline: Tech Entrepreneur & Author
sources.post_summary: Analyzes the convergence of AI agents and developer tools in automating DevOps pipelines. Discusses tools like Replicate and Hugging Face for rapid prototyping. Warns about ethical considerations in fully autonomous systems.

sources.author: Sami Rahman
sources.headline: Founder @ Overloop | Helping RevOps & Sales leaders scale outbound with AI
sources.post_summary: Discusses building a GTM engineering function from scratch, emphasizing the need for specialized engineers who bridge product, sales, and marketing. Shares a framework for hiring and structuring GTM teams to accelerate revenue growth. Highlights real-world results from implementing outbound automation pipelines.

sources.author: Kyle Coleman
sources.headline: Head of Sales Operations @ Gong | Revenue Operations Expert
sources.post_summary: Outlines a step-by-step guide to outbound automation using AI tools, reducing manual prospecting by 70%. Covers integration with CRM systems and key metrics for sales ops success. Warns against common pitfalls in scaling automated outreach.

sources.author: Amy Volas
sources.headline: CEO @ Avenue Talent Partners | RevOps & Sales Leadership
sources.post_summary: Explores the evolution of revenue operations in 2024, focusing on aligning sales ops, marketing ops, and customer success. Provides actionable strategies for content strategy to fuel GTM motions. Includes case studies on revops transformations driving 2x pipeline growth.

sources.author: Dan Disney
sources.headline: Keynote Speaker | Sales Leadership Coach
sources.post_summary: Breaks down content strategy for B2B sales teams, integrating it with outbound automation for higher conversion rates. Shares templates and examples for sales enablement content. Emphasizes data-driven iteration in revops contexts.

sources.author: Aasish Pappu
sources.headline: Co-founder & CTO @ Apollo.io | GTM Engineering Leader
sources.post_summary: Details the role of GTM engineering in modern SaaS, including custom automation for outbound and sales ops workflows. Advocates for engineering-led revenue operations to outpace competitors. References open-source tools and internal playbooks.

sources.author: Arvid Kahl
sources.headline: Founder, Author, bootstrapped two companies to $1M+ ARR
sources.post_summary: Arvid shares his journey as a solo founder shipping his latest SaaS product after bootstrapping previous exits. He details the tech stack, challenges faced during development, and initial user feedback from building in public. Emphasizes the power of consistent shipping over perfection.

sources.author: KP (Karthik Pattabiraman)
sources.headline: Solo Developer & Indie Hacker
sources.post_summary: KP recounts launching his AI-powered tool as a solo dev, from idea to MVP in 30 days. He breaks down the revenue numbers in week one and lessons on marketing without a team. Building in public helped him iterate based on community input.

sources.author: Sahil Lavingia
sources.headline: Founder & CEO at Gumroad
sources.post_summary: Sahil reflects on shipping new features to Gumroad while maintaining transparency with his audience. He discusses pivoting from VC-backed to bootstrapped model and the freedom it brings for solo-like building. Shares metrics on recent product updates.

sources.author: Addy Osmani
sources.headline: Engineering Leader at Google
sources.post_summary: Addy shares his career pivot from engineering to leadership while side-building open-source tools. He advises on transitioning into tech from non-traditional backgrounds and balancing full-time work with shipping personal projects publicly.

sources.author: Pieter Levels
sources.headline: Founder of Nomad List, Remote OK
sources.post_summary: Pieter updates on shipping a new product as a one-man army, including live revenue stats and dev logs. He highlights the efficiency of solo development and rapid iteration based on public feedback loops.
